name: Deep-Dish Rhubarb-Strawberry Pie
recipeIngredient: Flaky Pastry II (or 1/2-package pie crust mix)
recipeInstructions:
1. Sift flour and salt into a medium bowl; cut in shortening with a fork or pastry blender until mixture is crumbly.
2. Sprinkle cold water over the mixture, about 1 tablespoon at a time; mix lightly with a fork just until the pastry holds together and leaves sides of bowl clean. Make a ball; flatten it. Wrap dough in plastic and store in refrigerator until ready for use.
3. Mix sugar, flour, cinnamon, and cloves in a bowl.
4. Wash rhubarb; trim ends; cut into 1-inch pieces (You should have about 6 cups). Wash strawberries; hull and halve (you should have about 4 cups).
5. Place both in a large bowl. Sprinkle with sugar mixture; toss lightly to mix. Let stand 15 minutes.
6. Toss again.
7. Spoon rhubarb-strawberry filling into an 8 x 8 x 2-inch baking dish; dot with butter or margarine.
8. Prepare pastry by rolling out to a 10-inch square on a lightly floured surface. Cut into 1/2-inch strips with a pastry wheel or knife.
9. Weave strips into a lattice and cover filling. Turn ends under just enough so that strips touch sides of baking dish.
10. Brush lattice top with milk or cream; sprinkle with sugar.
11. Bake in hot oven (425°F) for 40 minutes, or until pastry is golden and juices bubble up. Cool. Serve warm with vanilla ice cream, if desired.
